vibe: Operator-focused, dense, trustworthy, calm watchful, Bloomberg terminal energy

layout_and_spacing.grid_system: Control Room Grid (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4 for dense dashboards)
layout_and_spacing.borders: Visible skeleton. Use sharp distinct cards.
layout_and_spacing.shadows: None. Use 1px borders to define hierarchy, not shadows.

components_strategy.tables: Dense. border-b border-zinc-800. JetBrains Mono for all numbers.
components_strategy.buttons: Square edges. Primary action is high contrast (bg-zinc-100 text-zinc-900). Secondary is outline (border border-zinc-700).
components_strategy.badges: px-2 py-0.5 text-xs font-mono uppercase border border-current bg-transparent
components_strategy.forms: Inputs should have sharp borders, dark backgrounds, and bright focus rings.

key_features.observation_banner: Fixed top bar. bg-amber-400 text-black font-mono text-xs uppercase font-bold text-center py-1.5 tracking-widest. Content: 'OBSERVATION ONLY MODE — BROKER_LIVE_ORDER_ENABLED=false'
key_features.runtime_cards: Each runtime (Alpha, Camaro, Chevelle) gets a dedicated status card. Use a top border (border-t-2) matching their accent color.
key_features.diagnostics_panel: Log-style viewer for mongo connectivity, health, and receipts.
key_features.navigation: Minimal top nav. Focus should be on the dashboard data.

instructions_to_main_agent: Apply Archetype 4 (Swiss/Terminal) strictly in DARK MODE. Backgrounds should be black/deep zinc.
Use JetBrains Mono for almost everything to simulate a quant/trading environment. Use Chivo for large headers only.
Color-code Alpha (Blue), Camaro (Amber), Chevelle (Green) in their respective UI zones using borders or text colors.
Build the dashboard as a dense 'Mission Control'. No empty whitespace for the sake of it. Pack the data cleanly.
Ensure 'data-testid' attributes are on all interactive elements.
Do NOT use Shadcn Card component if it forces rounded corners and shadows. Build custom sharp cards using Tailwind.
Include the observation banner prominently at the top of the application.
